finals.title: WORDPLAY FINAL
finals.clue: This 4-digit year saw Kraftwerk score their first UK number one, the French animated film Amélie debut in cinemas, and Canada celebrate its pacemaker inventor Wilson Greatbatch — but only one of those events actually happened in this year. Which year did Amélie first release in cinemas?
finals.fit_justification: Source answer '2001' is a 4-digit year; source question asks 'In which year was Amélie first released in the cinema?' The clue uses the year-answer structural theme of the pool.

finals.title: WORDPLAY FINAL
finals.clue: The source question for this fact literally asks for a '3-letter word that can precede cord, current, off, and saw' — making it the ultimate wordplay clue in disguise.
finals.fit_justification: Source question literally states 'What 3-letter word can precede cord, current, off, and saw?' — a pure wordplay puzzle whose answer 'Rip' is exactly 3 letters and solves all four compound words (ripcord, rip current, rip off, ripsaw).
